info.name: Ranger
info.description: The Ranger additions to the Realms of Indoril game series.

description.p: Update your class to be compatible with the Realms of Indoril series of games. This will replace certain class features, add new ones, and augment existing ones.
If you take a subclass that grants Spellcasting, the number of spells you may prepare increases by 2
Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

sheet.description: Test You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

description.p: Starting at 13th level, your Spellwoven Strikes become deadlier.
When you hit a creature with Spellwoven Strike, you have advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the end of your next turn.
In addition, the extra damage dealt by your Spellwoven Strike increases to 1d10 per level of the spell slot expended, instead of 1d6.
When you hit a creature with Spellwoven Strike, you have advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the end of your next turn.
In addition, the extra damage dealt by your Spellwoven Strike increases to 1d10 per level of the spell slot expended, instead of 1d6.
Starting at 3rd level, you can weave primal magic through your weapons, turning each strike into a specialized technique.
When you finish a long rest, choose four Spellwoven Strike options from the list below. These are your prepared Spellwoven Strikes until you finish your next long rest.
Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one Ranger spell slot to empower the attack with one of your prepared Spellwoven Strikes.
When you do so, apply the chosen Spellwoven Strike's effect. If an option requires a saving throw, the saving throw DC equals your ranger spell save DC.
You can use Spellwoven Strike a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
You have 4 chosen Spellwoven Strike effects.
Thorns or hooked barbs grow from your weapon.
The target takes extra piercing damage equal to 1d6 per level of the spell slot expended.
Until the start of your next turn, the first time the target willingly moves 5 feet or more, it takes piercing damage equal to your Wisdom modifier.
The target takes extra piercing damage equal to 1d{{spellwoven-strike:dice}} per level of the spell slot expended.
Until the start of your next turn, the first time the target willingly moves 5 feet or more, it takes piercing damage equal to your Wisdom modifier
Vines or spectral cords lash out from your weapon.
The target takes extra damage equal to 1d6 per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the target is restrained until the end of your next turn. This has no effect on huge or gargantuan creatures unless hit successfully with two consecutive binding weaves.
At the end of each of its turns, the target can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.
The target takes extra damage equal to 1d{{spellwoven-strike:dice}} per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the target is restrained until the end of your next turn. This has no effect on huge or gargantuan creatures unless hit successfully with two consecutive binding weaves.
At the end of each of its turns, the target can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Your weapon strikes with crushing force.
The target takes extra bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the target falls prone and cannot stand until the end of its next turn.
The target takes extra bludgeoning damage equal to 1d{{spellwoven-strike:dice}} per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the target falls prone and cannot stand until the end of its next turn.
Your weapon carries weakening venom.
The target takes extra necrotic damage equal to 1d6 per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Constitution saving throw.
On a failed save, the target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.
Your weapon carries weakening venom.
The target takes extra necrotic damage equal to 1d{{spellwoven-strike:dice}} per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Constitution saving throw.
On a failed save, the target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.
Roots, ice, or spectral claws anchor the target.
The target takes extra damage equal to 1d6 per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the target's speed becomes 0 until the start of your next turn.
The target takes extra damage equal to 1d{{spellwoven-strike:dice}} per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the target's speed becomes 0 until the start of your next turn.
Your weapon hits with a blast of force.
The target takes extra force damage equal to 1d6 per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, you push the target up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.
The target takes extra force damage equal to 1d{{spellwoven-strike:dice}} per level of the spell slot expended, and it must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, you push the target up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.
This Spellwoven Strike can only be used with a ranged weapon attack.
Your projectile erupts with elemental force.
The target takes extra damage equal to 1d6 per level of the spell slot expended. Choose the damage type when you use this option: cold, fire, lightning, thunder, or force.
Each creature of your choice within 10 feet of the target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage of the chosen type equal to your Wisdom modifier.
This Spellwoven Strike can only be used with a ranged weapon attack.
Your projectile erupts with elemental force.
The target takes extra damage equal to 1d{{spellwoven-strike:dice}} per level of the spell slot expended. Choose the damage type when you use this option: cold, fire, lightning, thunder, or force.
Each creature of your choice within 10 feet of the target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage of the chosen type equal to your Wisdom modifier.
